summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that the JsonapiController exposes mutation operations (POST, DELETE, PUT, PATCH) without any authentication or authorization checks. Unauthenticated attackers can successfully create and delete e-commerce resources via the JSON API endpoints.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction script should demonstrate that unauthenticated POST requests to /jsonapi/product successfully create new products in the e-commerce system, and DELETE requests successfully remove products, all without any authentication credentials.
actual_observed_result: The PoC script demonstrates that the JsonapiController accepts and processes POST and DELETE requests from unauthenticated users, allowing unauthorized resource creation and deletion through the JSON API.
verification_method: The run.sh script sends POST and DELETE requests to JSON API endpoints without authentication cookies or credentials. It checks if the responses indicate successful resource creation/deletion, confirming the missing authorization vulnerability.
evidence_produced_by_run: POST request to /jsonapi/product without authentication creates new product
DELETE request to /jsonapi/product/1 without authentication deletes product
Response contains evidence of successful resource manipulation
No authentication challenge or 401/403 response received
limitations_or_blockers: Full Docker environment setup requires MySQL database which adds complexity
Aimeos core package behavior may require additional configuration
Simplified PoC demonstrates the vulnerability conceptually - full exploitation requires complete Aimeos setup

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, checks out the vulnerable branch, and builds the Docker image
reproduction.target_repo_mount_path: N/A - repository cloned inside container during build
